title: Unlimited Permanent Residence
description: How to upgrade from 5-year permanent residence to unlimited — who qualifies and what you need

exercises.title: Key Terms
exercises.subtitle: These are the terms you will see on forms and hear at the Foreign Police.

scenarios.situation: You have held 5-year permanent residence for 3 years and 8 months. Can you apply for unlimited permanent residence now?
choices: Yes — close enough to 4 years
No — you need at least 4 full years of 5-year permanent residence
Yes — the minimum is 3 years
No — you must first apply for long-term EU residence
scenarios.answer: No — you need at least 4 full years of 5-year permanent residence
scenarios.answerMeaning: The condition is clear — at least 4 years of granted 5-year permanent residence immediately before applying. 3 years and 8 months does not qualify. Wait until the 4-year mark.

scenarios.situation: Where do you submit the application for unlimited permanent residence?
choices: At the Slovak embassy in your home country
At any police station in Slovakia
Only at the Foreign Police in Slovakia
By post to the Ministry of Interior
scenarios.answer: Only at the Foreign Police in Slovakia
scenarios.answerMeaning: Unlike the 5-year permit, the application for unlimited permanent residence can only be submitted in person at the Foreign Police in Slovakia. You cannot apply at a Slovak embassy abroad.

scenarios.situation: You arrive at the Foreign Police to submit your application. You realise you left your passport at home. What happens?
choices: The officer accepts the application and notes the missing passport
You can submit a photocopy for now and bring the original later
The Foreign Police will not accept the application without a valid passport
You can use your residence card instead
scenarios.answer: The Foreign Police will not accept the application without a valid passport
scenarios.answerMeaning: A valid travel document (cestovný doklad) is mandatory. If you do not present it, the Foreign Police will not accept the application at all. Always bring your original passport to any Foreign Police appointment.

scenarios.situation: You are 16 years old and your parent has unlimited permanent residence. Do you need to provide a criminal record check?
choices: No — criminal records are never required for children
Yes — criminal records are required for children aged 14 to 18
No — only adults over 18 need a criminal record
Yes — but only if you have previously been to court
scenarios.answer: Yes — criminal records are required for children aged 14 to 18
scenarios.answerMeaning: For children applying as dependants of a parent with unlimited permanent residence, a criminal record check (bezúhonnosť) is required if the child is between 14 and 18 years old. Under 14 — not required.

scenarios.situation: The officer asks for your proof of health insurance. The document you bring was issued 95 days ago. Will they accept it?
choices: Yes — health insurance documents have no expiry
Yes — 95 days is within the 6-month limit
No — supporting documents cannot be older than 90 days
Only if your insurer stamps it again
scenarios.answer: No — supporting documents cannot be older than 90 days
scenarios.answerMeaning: All supporting documents for the unlimited permanent residence application must not be older than 90 days. A document issued 95 days ago will be rejected. Request a fresh confirmation from your health insurer before your appointment.

exercises.title: At the Foreign Police — Key Phrases
exercises.subtitle: What you will say and hear at your unlimited permanent residence appointment.

rows.label: Opening
rows.slovak: Chcel/a by som požiadať o trvalý pobyt na neobmedzený čas.
rows.english: I would like to apply for unlimited permanent residence.

rows.label: Your status
rows.slovak: Mám trvalý pobyt na päť rokov štyri roky.
rows.english: I have had 5-year permanent residence for four years.

rows.label: Timeline
rows.english: When will the decision be ready?

rows.label: Accommodation
rows.english: Here is the proof of accommodation.

rows.label: Insurance
rows.slovak: Tu je potvrdenie o zdravotnom poistení.
rows.english: Here is the proof of health insurance.

rows.label: Not clear
rows.english: I don't understand. Can you write it down?

words.slovak: Chcel by som požiadať o trvalý pobyt na neobmedzený čas.
words.english: I would like to apply for unlimited permanent residence.

words.slovak: Mám trvalý pobyt na päť rokov štyri roky.
words.english: I have had 5-year permanent residence for four years.

words.slovak: Tu je potvrdenie o zdravotnom poistení.
words.english: Here is the proof of health insurance.
When will the decision be ready?

items.sentence: Chcel by som požiadať o trvalý pobyt na ___ čas.
items.translation: I would like to apply for permanent residence for ___ time.
choices: obmedzený
päťročný
neobmedzený
dočasný

items.sentence: Na udelenie trvalého pobytu na neobmedzený čas musím mať trvalý pobyt na päť rokov najmenej ___ roky.
items.translation: To be granted unlimited permanent residence I must have had 5-year permanent residence for at least ___ years.
choices: dva
tri
štyri
päť

items.sentence: Žiadosť možno podať len na ___ útvare v SR.
items.translation: The application can only be submitted at the ___ unit in Slovakia.
choices: obecnom
colnom
policajnom
súdnom

items.sentence: Doklady priložené k žiadosti nesmú byť staršie ako ___ dní.
items.translation: Documents attached to the application must not be older than ___ days.

items.sentence: Bezúhonnosť sa vyžaduje od detí starších ako ___ rokov.
items.translation: A criminal record is required from children older than ___ years.

items.sentence: Policajný útvar rozhodne o žiadosti do ___ dní.
items.translation: The Foreign Police decides on the application within ___ days.

scenarios.situation: The officer says they may also request proof of accommodation and a sworn statement that your reasons for residence still apply. Are these documents always required?
choices: Yes — they are always mandatory
No — they may be requested by the officer but are not always required
No — accommodation proof is never needed for unlimited residence
Yes — but only for children
scenarios.answer: No — they may be requested by the officer but are not always required
scenarios.answerMeaning: The proof of accommodation and the sworn statement (čestné vyhlásenie) that your reasons for residence still apply are documents the officer may request — but they are not automatically mandatory for everyone. Bring them anyway to avoid a wasted trip.

scenarios.situation: Your landlord is willing to provide a sworn statement about your accommodation. What is an acceptable form of this document?
choices: A text message from your landlord
A written statement from your landlord confirming they provide accommodation to you
A utility bill in your name
A screenshot of your rental payment
scenarios.answer: A written statement from your landlord confirming they provide accommodation to you
scenarios.answerMeaning: An acceptable accommodation document can be a written sworn statement (čestné vyhlásenie) from the property owner or user confirming they provide accommodation, a lease contract, or a confirmation from a hotel or dormitory. Must not be older than 90 days.

scenarios.situation: You own the flat you live in. What do you provide as proof of accommodation?
choices: Nothing — owners do not need to prove accommodation
Your own sworn statement declaring ownership of the property
A letter from a neighbour confirming you live there
A bank statement showing mortgage payments
scenarios.answer: Your own sworn statement declaring ownership of the property
scenarios.answerMeaning: If you own the property, you can provide your own sworn statement (čestné vyhlásenie) declaring ownership. You may also need to show proof of your ownership rights such as a property register extract.

scenarios.situation: After how many years of unlimited permanent residence can a foreigner generally begin exploring Slovak citizenship?
choices: Immediately after getting unlimited permanent residence
After 3 years of unlimited permanent residence
After 5 years of unlimited permanent residence
Unlimited permanent residence does not lead to citizenship
scenarios.answer: After 5 years of unlimited permanent residence
scenarios.answerMeaning: Generally a foreigner can apply for Slovak citizenship after 8 years of legal residence in total, including at least 5 years of permanent residence (any type). Unlimited permanent residence is the most stable foundation for this path. Always verify current rules at minv.sk.

scenarios.situation: You submit a complete application. How long can the Foreign Police take to make a decision?
choices: 30 days
60 days
90 days
180 days
scenarios.answerMeaning: The Foreign Police must decide on the application within 90 days of receiving it. You can legally remain in Slovakia while waiting for the decision once a complete application has been submitted.

exercises.context: You are applying for unlimited permanent residence. The Foreign Police may request proof of accommodation. You need your landlord, Pán Novák, to prepare a written statement confirming he provides accommodation to you. You are texting him.

A.title: Statement confirmed! ✅
A.description: Pán Novák will prepare the accommodation statement and have it ready before your appointment.

B.title: You got all the details right! ✅
B.description: You confirmed the document must not be older than 90 days and cover your current address.

nodes.speaker: user
nodes.prompt: Start the conversation politely. How do you greet your landlord?

choices.text: Dobrý deň. Ospravedlňujem sa za vyrušenie.
choices.translation: Good day. I apologise for the disturbance.

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.translation: Good day. What do you need?
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: Hmm... what are you talking about?

choices.text: Žiadam o trvalý pobyt. Potrebujem doklad o ubytovaní.
choices.translation: I am applying for permanent residence. I need proof of accommodation.

choices.text: Potrebujem od vás čestné vyhlásenie pre cudzineckú políciu.
choices.translation: I need a sworn statement from you for the Foreign Police.
I want to lower the rent.

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.translation: I understand. What document exactly do you need?
nodes.wrongAnswerResponse: Hmm... to nie je odpoveď na moju otázku.
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: Hmm... that's not an answer to my question.

choices.text: Čestné vyhlásenie, že mi poskytujete ubytovanie na tejto adrese.
choices.translation: A sworn statement that you provide accommodation to me at this address.

choices.text: Písomné potvrdenie o ubytovaní podpísané vami.
choices.translation: A written confirmation of accommodation signed by you.
A copy of your rental contract.

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.text: Dobre. Musí byť nejako overené? Notárom?
nodes.translation: Alright. Does it need to be certified? By a notary?
nodes.wrongAnswerTranslation: Sorry... I don't understand your answer.

choices.text: Nie, stačí váš podpis. Ale nesmie byť staršie ako 90 dní.
choices.translation: No, your signature is enough. But it must not be older than 90 days.

choices.text: Nie je potrebný notár. Váš podpis postačuje.
choices.translation: A notary is not needed. Your signature is sufficient.

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.translation: Understood. When do you need it?

choices.text: Čím skôr. Mám stretnutie budúci týždeň.
choices.translation: As soon as possible. I have an appointment next week.

choices.text: Do piatku, ak je to možné.

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.text: Žiadny problém. Pripravím to do zajtra.
nodes.translation: No problem. I will have it ready by tomorrow.

nodes.speaker: landlord
nodes.text: Mám uviesť vašu presnú adresu v dokumente?
nodes.translation: Should I include your exact address in the document?

choices.text: Áno, prosím. Uveďte presnú adresu a dátum.
choices.translation: Yes please. Include the exact address and date.

choices.text: Áno — adresu, dátum a váš podpis.
choices.translation: Yes — the address, date and your signature.
No, the address is not needed.

questions.question: What is a 'čestné vyhlásenie'?
choices: A certified translation
A statutory declaration / sworn statement
A criminal record check
A lease contract
questions.explanation: Čestné vyhlásenie literally means 'honourable declaration'. It is a written signed statement declaring something to be true. For accommodation purposes it is signed by the property owner or provider and confirms they are providing housing to the applicant.

questions.question: Why did you tell the landlord the document must not be older than 90 days?
choices: Because landlords tend to use old documents
Because all supporting documents for this application cannot be older than 90 days
Because the Foreign Police only checks documents from the last 3 months
Because health insurance documents expire after 90 days
questions.answer: Because all supporting documents for this application cannot be older than 90 days
questions.explanation: The 90-day rule applies to all supporting documents for the unlimited permanent residence application. If the landlord's statement is older than 90 days when you submit it, the officer will reject it.

questions.question: Does the landlord's accommodation statement need to be notarised?
choices: Yes — always required
No — a signed written statement is sufficient
Only if the landlord is a company
Only if the property is rented not owned
questions.answer: No — a signed written statement is sufficient
questions.explanation: According to the MIC guidance, a written signed statement (čestné vyhlásenie) from the property owner or provider is accepted. Notarisation is not explicitly required — though always confirm with the Foreign Police as requirements can vary.

questions.question: What key details should the landlord include in the accommodation statement?
choices: Only their name and signature
The rental amount and bank details
The exact address, date, and signature
The duration of your original lease
questions.answer: The exact address, date, and signature
questions.explanation: The statement should clearly state the address where accommodation is provided, the date it was written (to confirm the 90-day validity), and the landlord's signature. These are the minimum details needed for the Foreign Police.

scenarios.situation: You have held 5-year permanent residence for exactly 4 years. You submit your application for unlimited permanent residence today. Two months later you receive a decision. Is everything in order?
choices: No — you needed to wait longer before applying
Yes — you met the 4-year condition and the decision came within 90 days
No — the decision should have come within 30 days
Yes — but only if you also held temporary residence before
scenarios.answer: Yes — you met the 4-year condition and the decision came within 90 days
scenarios.answerMeaning: You qualified at the 4-year mark and the decision came within the 90-day legal deadline. Everything is correct.

scenarios.situation: Your child is 12 years old and qualifies for unlimited permanent residence through you. Do they need to provide a criminal record?
choices: Yes — always required regardless of age
No — criminal records are only required for children aged 14 to 18
Yes — but only from Slovakia
No — children never need criminal records
scenarios.answer: No — criminal records are only required for children aged 14 to 18
scenarios.answerMeaning: The criminal record check for dependant children only applies to those aged 14 to 18. Your 12-year-old child does not need one.

scenarios.situation: You are preparing your documents. Your health insurance confirmation is from 85 days ago. Your proof of accommodation is from 95 days ago. Which is a problem?
choices: Both are fine
Both are a problem
Only the health insurance — 85 days is too old
Only the accommodation proof — 95 days exceeds the 90-day limit
scenarios.answer: Only the accommodation proof — 95 days exceeds the 90-day limit
scenarios.answerMeaning: The health insurance confirmation at 85 days is within the 90-day limit — fine. The accommodation proof at 95 days exceeds the limit — you need a fresh one from your landlord.

scenarios.situation: You want to apply but are unsure if the Foreign Police will ask for your accommodation proof. What should you do?
choices: Do not bring it — wait until they ask
Bring it anyway — it may be requested and a wasted trip is avoidable
Call the Foreign Police to ask first
Only bring it if your landlord insists
scenarios.answer: Bring it anyway — it may be requested and a wasted trip is avoidable
scenarios.answerMeaning: The accommodation proof and the sworn statement may or may not be requested — but bringing them costs nothing. Being turned away for a missing document and having to rebook wastes weeks. Always come over-prepared.

referenceCard.title: Unlimited Permanent Residence — Reference Card
referenceCard.titleSlovak: Trvalý pobyt na neobmedzený čas — Referenčná karta
referenceCard.unlockedMessage: 🎉 You completed the Unlimited Permanent Residence lesson! Your reference card is now saved to your profile.

checklist.item: Completed application form (official MV SR form)

checklist.warning: Mandatory — application not accepted without it. Bring original.

checklist.item: Proof of health insurance in Slovakia
checklist.warning: Must not be older than 90 days
Must not be older than 90 days. Not required for children under 14.
Must not be older than 90 days. Bring it anyway — better safe than a wasted trip.

checklist.item: Sworn statement that reasons for residence still apply (may be requested)
checklist.slovak: Čestné vyhlásenie o trvaní dôvodov pobytu
checklist.warning: May be requested by the officer. Prepare in advance.

keyFacts: You must have held 5-year permanent residence for at least 4 years before applying
Application must be submitted in person at the Foreign Police in Slovakia — not at an embassy
No valid passport = application not accepted
All supporting documents must not be older than 90 days
The unlimited residence card does not expire — no periodic renewal needed
Children under 14 do not need a criminal record check; children aged 14–18 do

keyPhrase.english: I would like to apply for unlimited permanent residence.
keyPhrase.slovak: Chcel/a by som požiadať o trvalý pobyt na neobmedzený čas.

referenceCard.freeHelp: IOM Migration Information Centre: mic.iom.sk — free legal advice for foreigners
